definitions.corpus luteum granulosa theca layer: A cellular layer of the corpus luteum comprising both granulosa-lutein cells (large luteal cells derived from follicular granulosa cells, located centrally and arranged in cords around vascular spaces) and theca-lutein cells (small luteal cells derived from theca interna cells, located peripherally between the folds of the granulosa-lutein cell layer), which together form the principal steroidogenic parenchyma of the corpus luteum and cooperate in the biosynthesis of progesterone and estrogen.
